what: Evolutionarily Distinct and Globally Endangered calculator
title: Evolutionarily Distinct and Globally Endangered calculator
text: Package calculate EDGE scores phylogenetic-based indicators R  Documentation website current development…

title: Evolutionarily Distinct and Globally Endangered calculator
text: EDGE index metric aims prioritize species’ conservation bsed theis phylogenetic singularity (.e. ED, Evolutionaty Distinctiveness) extinction risk (GE, Globally Endangered). Based idea, several methods developed order calculate individual EDGE scores. illustrate differences among methods ant implementationn within rEDGE package, see examples using monotremates (.e. platypus echidna species).

title: Evolutionarily Distinct and Globally Endangered calculator
text: Index based Isaac et al., 2007. first approach used evolutionary distinctiveness (ED) transformation extinction risk (GE) IUCN Red List category twice probable becoming extinct. EDGE score calculated ussing following formula: EDGE = log(1+ED)+GE∗log(2) calculate index monotremates, just use calculate_EDGE1 function specifying uot phylogenetic tree, table including tree’s species (clomn named species, respective IUCN category column named RL.cat). Note species must included, species included tree evaluated, make sure include table wit “NE” category.
code: EDGE1 <- calculate_EDGE1(tree = monotreme.tree,                           table = monotreme.table,                           sort.list = T # to get the list sorted by decreasing EDGE value                          )  knitr::kable(EDGE1)

title: Evolutionarily Distinct and Globally Endangered calculator
text: order take account extinction probability closely related taxa well uncertainty extinction probability species, Gumbs et al., 2023 designed new methodology, termed EDGE2. new approach, probabilty extinction sampled continuous distribution based IUCN Red List category, ED2 EDGE” values can calculated (develop thoroughly…). EDGE score iteration dependant (.e. random factor sampling extinction probabilty), set EDGE2 values can calculated averaged posteriorly. multiple calculation performed calculate_EDGE2_multiple function, allows parallelize order speed computation times. example calculating EDGE scores 50 times averaging results .
code: EDGE2 <- calculate_EDGE2(tree = monotreme.tree,                           table = monotreme.table,                           sort.list = T, # to get the list sorted by decreasing EDGE value                          verbose = F)  knitr::kable(EDGE2) EDGE2mult <- calculate_EDGE2_multiple(tree = monotreme.tree,                                        table = monotreme.table,                                        n.iter = 50,                                       parallelize = TRUE,                                       n.cores = 10                                       ) #> [1] "Seed has been set to: 74593047"   # Now we summarise table results... EDGE2mult_summ <- EDGE2mult |>    bind_rows()  |>    group_by(species) |>    summarise(RL.cat = paste0(unique(RL.cat)),             TBL  = mean(TBL ),             pext = mean(pext),             ED   = mean(ED  ),             EDGE = mean(EDGE) ) |>    arrange(desc(EDGE))  knitr::kable(EDGE2mult_summ)

text: tree phylo object. table tibble data frame two columns, named "species" "RL.cat" method Method used. either "EDGE1" "EDGE2". output result running calculate_EDGE1, calculate_EDGE2 depending method specified.

title: EDGE2 calculating function. — calculate_EDGE2
text: tree phylo object. table tibble data frame two columns, named "species" "RL.cat" ext.prob Extinction probability associated category, based Isaac et al. (2007) Mooers et al. (2008). verbose Logical. progress printed . sort.list Logical. TRUE, EDGE list sorted higher lower values. return.Logical. TRUE, EDGE list, tree ePDloss list returned. FALSE (default), list returned.

title: EDGE2 calculating function. — calculate_EDGE2
text: list containing 3 slots `return.` TRUE. , first item (EDGE list) returned. list: output specific TBL (terminal branch length), pext (sampled probability extinction), ED (evolutionary distinctiveness), EDGE (EDGE index). tree: input tree. ePDloss: complete PD (phylogenetic diversity) ePDloss (expected PD loss). Units input tree  (generally Million years).

title: EDGE2 calculating function. — calculate_EDGE2
text: Isaac, N.J.B., Turvey, S.T., Collen, B., Waterman, C., Baillie, J.E.M. (2007). Mammals EDGE: Conservation Priorities Based Threat Phylogeny. PloS one, 2(3), e296. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0000296 Mooers, .Ø., Faith, D. P., & Maddison, W. P. (2008).  Converting endangered species categories probabilities extinction phylogenetic  conservation prioritization. PloS one, 3(11), e3700. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0003700 Gumbs, R., Gray, C. L., Böhm, M., Burfield, . J., Couchman, O. R., Faith, D. P., Forest, F., Hoffmann, M., #' Isaac, N. J. B., Jetz, W., Mace, G. M., Mooers, . O., Safi, K., Scott, O., Steel, M., Tucker, C. M., Pearse, W. D., Owen, N. R. & Rosindell, J. (2023). EDGE2 protocol: Advancing prioritisation Evolutionarily Distinct Globally Endangered species practical conservation action. PLoS Biology, 21(2), e3001991. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.3001991

title: Multiple iteration EDGE2 calculating function. — calculate_EDGE2_multiple
text: tree phylo object. table tibble data frame two columns, named "species" "RL.cat" verbose Logical. progress printed . sort.list Logical. TRUE, EDGE list sorted higher lower values. return.Logical. TRUE, EDGE list, tree ePDloss list returned. FALSE (default), list returned. n.iter Integer. Number times function run. parallelize Logical. TRUE, several CPU cores used compute EDGE scores. n.cores Integer. Number cores use simultaneoulsly. Default number available ones minus one. seed Integer. seed number used randomize iterations. NULL, random number enerated printed
